title: malus britannica
intro: Malus britannica, also known as the wild crab apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Malus britannica include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Malus britannica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
